steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context. The decisive clue is the word "but": the basking shark was classified as a full ectotherm, yet its body temperature stays above ambient water temperature. A full ectotherm by definition matches ambient temperature, so the finding contradicts the classification. The blank must mean "incompatible with" or "at odds with."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "incongruous with": means not in harmony or incompatible. An elevated body temperature cannot coexist with a "full ectotherm" classification, which requires temperature to match surroundings — the two are directly incompatible. ✓
  • "subordinate to": means lower in rank or secondary to. This implies a hierarchy between the finding and the classification, not the contradiction the passage describes. ✗
  • "opportune for": means well-timed or advantageous for. The elevated temperature does not benefit or suit the ectotherm classification — it conflicts with it. ✗
  • "indicative of": means serving as a sign of or pointing toward. An elevated body temperature would indicate endothermy or regional endothermy, not a full ectotherm classification — this has causation backwards. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
